tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: I'd like to save my writing preferences. I prefer fast-paced, character-driven stories with sharp dialogue and vivid world-building. I'm not big on heavy-handed messaging—I'd rather entertain while exploring big ideas.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "User's writing preferences: enjoys fast-paced, character-driven stories with sharp dialogue and vivid world-building. Prefers entertainment over heavy-handed messaging - likes to explore big ideas while being entertained."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "User's writing preferences: enjoys fast-paced, character-driven stories with sharp dialogue and vivid world-building. Prefers entertainment over heavy-handed messaging - likes to explore big ideas while being entertained."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content: Done! Your writing preferences are now saved. I'll keep in mind you enjoy fast-paced, character-driven stories with sharp dialogue and vivid world-building, and that you prefer entertainment with subtle exploration of big ideas over heavy-handed messaging.
Can you show me what memories you've stored so far? I want to make sure my preferences were saved correctly.

{"memories": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "User's writing preferences: enjoys fast-paced, character-driven stories with sharp dialogue and vivid world-building. Prefers entertainment over heavy-handed messaging - likes to explore big ideas while being entertained."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Here's what's currently stored:

1. **Writing Preferences** (ID: m2)
   - Enjoys fast-paced, character-driven stories with sharp dialogue and vivid world-building
   - Prefers entertainment over heavy-handed messaging—likes to explore big ideas while being entertained

Your preferences were saved correctly. This matches exactly what you told me. Want me to add anything else, or make any changes?

Yeah, actually I'd like to add a few more things. I tend to gravitate toward space opera and hard sci-fi themes—stuff with interesting technology concepts and exploration of alien cultures. I also really enjoy morally complex characters and stories where the line between hero and villain gets blurry. And I prefer endings that feel earned but aren't always perfectly tidy—I'd rather leave some questions open for readers to think about.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "Additional user writing preferences: gravitates toward space opera and hard sci-fi themes with interesting technology concepts and exploration of alien cultures. Enjoys morally complex characters and stories where the line between hero and villain gets blurry. Prefers endings that feel earned but aren't perfectly tidy—likes leaving some questions open for readers to think about."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m3", "user": "None", "text": "Additional user writing preferences: gravitates toward space opera and hard sci-fi themes with interesting technology concepts and exploration of alien cultures. Enjoys morally complex characters and stories where the line between hero and villain gets blurry. Prefers endings that feel earned but aren't perfectly tidy—likes leaving some questions open for readers to think about."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content:
Got it! I've added those preferences. Here's your updated list:

1. **Writing Preferences** (ID: m2)
   - Fast-paced, character-driven stories with sharp dialogue and vivid world-building
   - Prefers entertainment over heavy-handed messaging—likes exploring big ideas while being entertained

2. **Additional Preferences** (ID: m3)
   - Space opera and hard sci-fi with interesting tech concepts and alien cultures
   - Morally complex characters; blurred lines between hero and villain
   - Earned but untidy endings—likes open questions for readers

Anything else you'd like to add?
Great, that looks like everything. Thanks for showing me the full list—my preferences are all there and saved correctly. I think we're good to go.

You're welcome! Your preferences are all saved and ready. Whenever you want help with writing—whether it's brainstorming, feedback, or anything else—just let me know. Happy to help bring those space opera vibes and morally complex characters to life!
